document_metadata.bylaw_name: Land Use By-law for Beaver Bank, Hammonds Plains and Upper Sackville

permitted_uses.use_name: Multiple unit dwellings containing 3 or 4 dwelling units within the Urban Service Area (RC-May 23/24;E-June 13/24)
Shared housing use with 10 or fewer bedrooms in conjunction with a permitted dwelling unit (RC-Aug 9/22;E-Sep 15/22)
Day care facilities for not more than fourteen (14) children and in conjunction with permitted dwellings
Business uses in conjunction with permitted dwellings

provisions.text: R-3 ZONE REQUIREMENTS In any R -3 Zone, no development permit shall be issued except in conformity with the following: Minimum Lot Area 29,064 square feet (2700 m2) Minimum Frontage 100 feet (30.5 m) Minimum Front or Flankage Yard 20 feet (6.1 m) Minimum Rear or Side Yard 8 feet (2.4 m) Maximum Lot Coverage 35 per cent Maximum Height of Main Building 35 feet (10.7 m)

requirement_sections.title_label_raw: OTHER REQUIREMENTS: BUSINESS AND DAY CARE USES

provisions.text: OTHER REQUIREMENTS: BUSINESS AND DAY CARE USES Where business uses and day care facilities are permitted in any R-3 Zone, the provisions of Parts 11.3 and 11.4 shall apply.

content_blocks.text: No development permit shall be issued in any R -3 (Mobile Dwelling Subdivision) Zone
In any R -3 Zone, no development permit shall be issued except in conformity with the

Minimum Lot Area 29,064 square feet (2700 m2) Minimum Frontage 100 feet (30.5 m) Minimum Front or Flankage Yard 20 feet (6.1 m) Minimum Rear or Side Yard 8 feet (2.4 m) Maximum Lot Coverage 35 per cent Maximum Height of Main Building 35 feet (10.7 m)

content_blocks.heading_context_raw: 9.3 OTHER REQUIREMENTS: BUSINESS AND DAY CARE USES
content_blocks.text: Where business uses and day care facilities are permitted in any R-3 Zone, the provisions of Parts 11.3 and 11.4 shall apply.

open_issues.description: Clause patterns listed in pending_review_clause_patterns were preserved raw because their hierarchy normalization is not yet approved in this repository context.
